tools.name: intelligent_search_pubmed
tools.description: Context-aware PubMed search with automatic query enhancement and structured results optimized for LLM analysis

query.description: Natural language research query (e.g., 'EEG biomarkers for ADHD diagnosis in children', 'recent COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness studies')

max_results.description: Maximum articles to return with full analysis

focus_area.description: Research focus to optimize search strategy

include_context.description: Include query analysis and search context in response

tools.name: search_pubmed
tools.description: Traditional PubMed search for direct query control

email.description: Contact email (optional, uses default if not provided)

term.description: Search term, query, or keywords (e.g., 'covid vaccine', 'BRCA1 mutations')

retmax.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-1000)

retstart.description: Starting position for results (0-based, for pagination)

tools.name: get_pubmed_details
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about specific PubMed articles

email.description: Contact email (optional, uses default if not provided)

id.description: PubMed ID(s) to retrieve details for

tools.name: get_pubmed_info
tools.description: Get general information about the PubMed database

findings.title: Parameter 'id' in 'get_pubmed_details' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'id'.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'search_pubmed.email'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
❌ Input validation error: 1 validation error for ESearchRequest
email
  value is not a valid email address: The display name contains invalid characters when not quoted: '(', ')', '>'. [type=value_error, input_value='<script>alert(1)</script>', input_type=str]
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'search_pubmed.term'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'get_pubmed_details.email'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.evidence:
❌ Input validation error: 1 validation error for EFetchRequest
email
  value is not a valid email address: The display name contains invalid characters when not quoted: '(', ')', '>'. [type=value_error, input_value='<script>alert(1)</script>', input_type=str]
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'get_pubmed_details.id'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: 'intelligent_search_pubmed.query' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'search_pubmed.term' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'get_pubmed_details.id' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 3 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
